name: Ándara Massif Trail
name_original: Macizo de Ándara – circular desde Bejes
description: This difficult 17.2-mile mountain biking route in Picos de Europa offers rugged terrain, panoramic views, and 4083 feet of climbing.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails in Desfiladero De La Hermida navigate a dramatic landscape characterized by towering limestone cliffs, some reaching up to 600 meters, carved by the Deva River. This region, situated at the eastern edge of the Picos de Europa National Park, offers significant elevation gains, with routes often featuring challenging climbs and rewarding descents. The terrain includes diverse ecosystems with cork oaks, Atlantic forest species, and holm oaks, providing varied riding environments. Best mountain bike trails in Desfiladero De La Hermida The most popular mountain bike trail is Ándara Massif Trail, a 17.2 miles (27.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete, offering substantial elevation changes through rugged mountain terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Peña Ventosa - Transcantábrica Loop, a difficult 22.7 miles (36.5 km) path. This route features extensive climbs and descents, providing panoramic views of the surrounding peaks and valleys. Local mountain bikers also love the Lebeña – Santa Catalina Viewpoint loop from Cillorigo de Liébana, a 23.1 miles (37.2 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including the Santa Catalina viewpoint, often completed in about 4 hours 8 minutes. Mountain biking in Desfiladero De La Hermida is defined by towering limestone walls, the Deva River, and the backdrop of the Picos de Europa. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate routes to challenging, difficult paths. The routes in Desfiladero De La Hermida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Desfiladero De La Hermida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Desfiladero De La Hermida?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails available in Desfiladero De La Hermida, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has recorded 54 tours in the region.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Desfiladero De La Hermida?
A: The trails in Desfiladero De La Hermida range from moderate to difficult. While there is one easy route, the majority of the 54 available tours are either moderate (23 routes) or difficult (30 routes), often featuring significant elevation gains and challenging terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Desfiladero De La Hermida?
A: You can expect a dramatic landscape characterized by towering limestone cliffs, some reaching up to 600 meters, carved by the Deva River. The terrain includes diverse ecosystems with cork oaks, Atlantic forest species, and holm oaks, providing varied riding environments. Many routes feature extensive climbs and rewarding descents, with some reaching up to 2,950 meters in elevation.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Desfiladero De La Hermida?
A: Yes, the region is known for its spectacular viewpoints. The famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/861319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views of the gorge, the Deva River, and the Picos de Europa. You can experience this on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20499159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Viewpoint loop from La Hermida&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking in Desfiladero De La Hermida?
A: Beyond the towering limestone walls and the Deva River, you'll encounter diverse ecosystems including cork oaks, Atlantic forest species like oaks and beeches, and holm oaks. The area is also a special protection area for birds, so you might spot eagles, vultures, and chamois. The backdrop of the Picos de Europa National Park is ever-present, offering majestic mountain vistas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the gorge has a rich history. You can find prehistoric paintings over 20,000 years old and ancient paths connecting charming villages. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Lebeña Church&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/861322" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Creatures of Mount Monzarco&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31866972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lebeña – Santa Catalina Viewpoint loop from Cillorigo de Liébana&lt;/a&gt; passes through varied landscapes with cultural significance.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Desfiladero De La Hermida offers 30 difficult-rated routes for experienced riders. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000414814/pena-ventosa-transcantabrica-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Ventosa - Transcantábrica Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 22.7-mile path with extensive climbs and descents, providing panoramic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169824420/andara-massif-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ándara Massif Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 17.2-mile route with substantial elevation changes through rugged mountain terrain.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails that offer a good balance of challenge and scenery?
A: Yes, there are 23 moderate routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20499159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Viewpoint loop from La Hermida&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.7-mile trail that offers rewarding views without the extreme difficulty of some other routes. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20499116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Potes – Concrete paths loop from Tama&lt;/a&gt;, a 18.2-mile route.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Desfiladero De La Hermida?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Desfiladero De La Hermida are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000414814/pena-ventosa-transcantabrica-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Ventosa - Transcantábrica Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31866972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lebeña – Santa Catalina Viewpoint loop from Cillorigo de Liébana&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20499159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Viewpoint loop from La Hermida&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time to visit for mountain biking in Desfiladero De La Hermida?
A: The region is generally suitable for outdoor activities for much of the year. However, considering the significant elevation gains and rugged terrain, spring and autumn often provide the most comfortable temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential snow or ice in winter at higher altitudes.

Q: Is parking available for mountain bikers in Desfiladero De La Hermida?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail vary, the N-621 road, which winds through the gorge, provides access to various starting points and villages where parking can typically be found. It's advisable to check specific route details for recommended parking locations.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the village of La Hermida itself offers amenities, including natural thermal waters for relaxation after a ride. The region also features charming villages and towns like Potes, which provide accommodation, cafes, and pubs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20499116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Potes – Concrete paths loop from Tama&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that brings you close to such facilities.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Desfiladero De La Hermida?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the dramatic landscapes, challenging climbs, rewarding descents, and the breathtaking views of the Picos de Europa and the Deva River gorge.
tips: Take in the beautiful views that await you if you continue along the path to the imposing Andara Massif. The viewpoint offers an impressive panorama of the town of Bejes and the Cuetu d'Aves peak in the back.
From the road to La Revuleta there are spectacular views of mountains, gorges and the charming town of Bejes at the foot of the mountain.
The path offers spectacular views of the rocky mountains that confirm the Picos de Europa National Park.

At the foot of Pica Mancondiú, at the end of the Canal de las Vacas, at a height of 1,725 metres, is the shelter known as the Ándara casetón. Unique in the Eastern Massif of the nine that are found throughout Picos de Europa. It has impressive views.

They have capacity for 20 people and the possibility of making a reservation at http://www.casetondeandara.com/

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult mountain bike ride, requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills. While some sections have been improved with concrete on steep parts, riders should expect rugged terrain, significant elevation changes, and potentially technical sections (S3-S6).

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features diverse terrain, predominantly unpaved paths and gravel sections. You'll encounter old mining tracks, some rocky portions, and segments with steep inclines where you might need to push your bike. Some formerly difficult downhill sections are now concreted, making them easier to navigate.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Bejes. You can typically find parking within or near the village to begin your ride. Specific trailhead information is usually available on local maps or through the komoot app.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this route is generally during the spring, summer, and early autumn months when the weather is more stable and the trails are clear of snow. Always check local weather conditions before heading out, as mountain weather can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers spectacular panoramic views. Key viewpoints include Mirador La Sotorraña and La Revuelta, both offering impressive vistas of Bejes and peaks like Cuetu d'Aves. You'll also pass the Casetón de Ándara Refuge at 1,725 meters, which provides a scenic break point and stunning views of the Picos de Europa Mountains.

faqs.question: Does this route pass through any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You will ride along parts of the Macizo de Ándara, Senda de la Reserva del Saja, and briefly touch upon [CIMA CA13] La Hoja - Salto de la Cabra and El Anillo de Picos (3 Macizos).

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail, and are there any specific rules?
faqs.answer: As this route is located within the Picos de Europa National Park, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always follow national park regulations regarding pets.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are typically required to access the trails within the Picos de Europa National Park for day use. However, it's always advisable to check the latest regulations from the park authority before your visit, especially for group activities or specific areas.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while mountain biking?
faqs.answer: You'll experience dramatic and varied scenery, from the rugged, towering peaks of the Picos de Europa to deep gorges and lush forests, such as the beech forest of Valdediezma. The landscape can sometimes appear almost lunar, and on clear days, views can extend as far as the Cantabrian Sea.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation in Bejes?
faqs.answer: Bejes is a charming village and typically offers basic amenities. You may find local cafes, pubs, or small guesthouses (casas rurales) for refreshments and accommodation before or after your ride. It's recommended to check availability in advance, especially during peak season.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach Bejes?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to Bejes can be limited, especially for carrying mountain bikes. It's advisable to research local bus services or private transfer options well in advance. Many visitors choose to drive to the village.
